content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
February 14, 1997
Steve Baker
Aaron Read & Associates
Eleventh & L Building
1127 - 1 1
th Street, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-97-077
Dear Mr. Baker:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions ofthe Political
Reform Act (the “Act”).
1 Since you are asking a question that is purely hypothetical we are
declining to issue assistance at this time. (Regulation 1 8329(b)(8)(D). Copy enclosed.) Ifyou
have a question regarding the present version of the Act please feel free to submit another request
for advice.
Ifyou have any questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
;i/L
4
;c
-7&
By:
Marte Castaios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:ak
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asked a hypothetical question regarding the Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not included in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to issue advice because the question posed was purely hypothetical in nature, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D). The FPPC invited the requester to submit a new request if they have a question regarding the present version of the Act.

The substantive legal question is not disclosed. The letter cites Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) as the basis for declining to provide advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester asked a hypothetical question regarding the Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not included in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to issue advice because the question posed was purely hypothetical in nature, citing Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D). The FPPC invited the requester to submit a new request if they have a question regarding the present version of the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on a hypothetical question regarding the Political Reform Act, explaining that only non-hypothetical questions are eligible for assistance under FPPC regulations.
